docs.text: SudoBot # The ultimate solution for discord server moderation. Invite SudoBot # Read the terms here and if you agree with those then you can submit an invite request here . Features # Strong automoderation tools like Anti-raid systems and spam filters Various message filters A lot of manual moderation tools Secure
docs.title: Home

docs.text: The ultimate solution for discord server moderation.
docs.title: SudoBot

docs.text: Read the terms here and if you agree with those then you can submit an invite request here .
docs.title: Invite SudoBot

docs.text: Strong automoderation tools like Anti-raid systems and spam filters Various message filters A lot of manual moderation tools Secure
docs.title: Features

docs.text: Moderation # SudoBot has these manual moderation tools: Banning/Kicking members Softbanning/tempbanning/massbanning members Muting and hardmuting members Clearing messages in bulk Warning users Sending DMs to users Locking/unlocking channels Taking notes about users
docs.title: Moderation

docs.text: SudoBot has these manual moderation tools: Banning/Kicking members Softbanning/tempbanning/massbanning members Muting and hardmuting members Clearing messages in bulk Warning users Sending DMs to users Locking/unlocking channels Taking notes about users
docs.title: Moderation

docs.text: Banning Users # Normal Ban # Use the -ban command to ban a user, regardless if they are in the server or not. Legacy Command # -ban <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [-d=DAYS] [Reason] Options : - -d : Specify the how many days old messages to delete. Must be in range of 0-9. Default is - "Don't delete any". Slash Command # /ban <User> [days] [reason] Examples # -ban @Someone -ban @Someone Spamming a lot -ban @Someone -d 5 Spamming a lot -ban 45643846466843 -ban 45643846466843 Spamming a lot -ban 45643846466843 -d 7 Spamming a lot Mass Ban # Use the -massban command to massban users. Legacy Command # -massban <...UserIDs|UserMentions> [Reason] Slash Command # /massban <...UserIDs> [days] [reason] Note While using slash command, the IDs must be separated with spaces. Examples # -massban 23626825621964 217216291276407 -massban 23626825621964 217216291276407 63876523582826865 Raiding the Server -massban 23626825621964 217216291276407 @Someone 36362742746674467 Unbanning # Use the -unban command to unban users. Legacy Command # -unban <UserID> Slash Command # /unban <UserID> Examples # -unban 7485692162169
docs.title: Banning Users

docs.text: Use the -ban command to ban a user, regardless if they are in the server or not.
docs.title: Normal Ban

docs.text: -ban <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [-d=DAYS] [Reason] Options : - -d : Specify the how many days old messages to delete. Must be in range of 0-9. Default is - "Don't delete any".
docs.title: Legacy Command

docs.text: /ban <User> [days] [reason]
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: -ban @Someone -ban @Someone Spamming a lot -ban @Someone -d 5 Spamming a lot -ban 45643846466843 -ban 45643846466843 Spamming a lot -ban 45643846466843 -d 7 Spamming a lot
docs.title: Examples

docs.text: Use the -massban command to massban users.
docs.title: Mass Ban

docs.text: -massban <...UserIDs|UserMentions> [Reason]
docs.title: Legacy Command

docs.text: /massban <...UserIDs> [days] [reason] Note While using slash command, the IDs must be separated with spaces.
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: Use the -unban command to unban users.
docs.title: Unbanning

docs.text: -unban <UserID>
docs.title: Legacy Command

docs.text: /unban <UserID>
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: Clearing messages in bulk # Sudobot has -clear command which can delete messages in bulk. Warning Messages deleted using this command won't be logged. Legacy Command # -clear <UserID|UserMention> Slash Command # /clear [User] [MessageCount] [Channel] Note While using slash command, you must specify either the message count or the user. Info This operation might take some time depending on the amount of messages. Examples # -clear @Someone -clear 347382275362482
docs.title: Clearing messages in bulk

docs.text: Sudobot has -clear command which can delete messages in bulk. Warning Messages deleted using this command won't be logged.
docs.title: Clearing messages in bulk

docs.text: -clear <UserID|UserMention>
docs.title: Legacy Command

docs.text: /clear [User] [MessageCount] [Channel] Note While using slash command, you must specify either the message count or the user. Info This operation might take some time depending on the amount of messages.
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: -clear @Someone -clear 347382275362482
docs.title: Examples

docs.text: Locking Channels # You can lock channels using -lock command. This sets a permission override to the target channel that denies SEND_MESSAGES permission for @everyone role. Warning If other roles have SEND_MESSAGES permission allowed then members having them could bypass the channel lock. You should take care about this. Legacy Command # -lock [ChannelID|ChannelMention] Slash Command # /lock [Channel] Note If the channel is not specified then the system will lock the current channel. Examples # -lock -lock 347382275362482 -lock #general Unlocking Channels # You can unlock channels back using -unlock command. This sets a permission override to the target channel that reverts the SEND_MESSAGES permission for @everyone role as it was before. Legacy Command # -unlock [ChannelID|ChannelMention] Slash Command # /unlock [Channel] Note If the channel is not specified then the system will unlock the current channel. Examples # -unlock -unlock 347382275362482 -unlock #general
docs.title: Locking Channels

docs.text: You can lock channels using -lock command. This sets a permission override to the target channel that denies SEND_MESSAGES permission for @everyone role. Warning If other roles have SEND_MESSAGES permission allowed then members having them could bypass the channel lock. You should take care about this.

docs.text: -lock -lock 347382275362482 -lock #general
docs.title: Examples

docs.text: You can unlock channels back using -unlock command. This sets a permission override to the target channel that reverts the SEND_MESSAGES permission for @everyone role as it was before.
docs.title: Unlocking Channels

docs.text: -unlock [ChannelID|ChannelMention]
docs.title: Legacy Command

docs.text: /unlock [Channel] Note If the channel is not specified then the system will unlock the current channel.
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: -unlock -unlock 347382275362482 -unlock #general
docs.title: Examples

docs.text: Muting members # Use the -mute command to mute members. This will assign a muted role to the member and each channel is expected to have a permission override for this role which denies the SEND_MESSAGES permission. Legacy Command # -mute <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [-t=TIME] [Reason] Slash Command # /mute <Member> [Time] [Reason] Examples # -mute @Someone -mute 347382275362482 -mute @Someone Spamming -mute @Someone -t 50m Spamming -mute 347382275362482 -t 12h Spamming Unmuting # Use the -unmute command to unmute members. Legacy Command # -unmute <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> Slash Command # /unmute <Member> Examples # -unmute @Someone -unmute 5256258251685246
docs.title: Muting members

docs.text: Use the -mute command to mute members. This will assign a muted role to the member and each channel is expected to have a permission override for this role which denies the SEND_MESSAGES permission.
docs.title: Muting members

docs.text: -mute <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [-t=TIME] [Reason]
docs.title: Legacy Command

docs.text: /mute <Member> [Time] [Reason]
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: -mute @Someone -mute 347382275362482 -mute @Someone Spamming -mute @Someone -t 50m Spamming -mute 347382275362482 -t 12h Spamming
docs.title: Examples

docs.text: Use the -unmute command to unmute members.
docs.title: Unmuting

docs.text: -unmute <UserID|UserMention|UserTag>
docs.title: Legacy Command

docs.text: /unmute <Member>
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: -unmute @Someone -unmute 5256258251685246
docs.title: Examples
